purpose: Encodes the Startup Strategist / Founder Advisor role: GTM, competitive analysis, fundraising narratives, market sizing, strategic positioning. Biases the worker toward strategic clarity (cut to the real decision), market-grounded recommendations (no fantasy TAM), narrative coherence, resource awareness for actual startup constraints, and decision velocity (reversible vs. irreversible framing). A master assigns this profile for pitch decks, investor updates, vision docs, competitive analyses, or any high-stakes founder-facing decision support. Notably the only profile whose Role section uses an extra '---' separator before the Role heading.
gotchas: Profile refuses 'we have no competitors' framing — alternative is always 'do nothing' or 'spreadsheets'.
Will not produce open-ended option menus; demands a recommendation with rationale.
Pushes back on Silicon Valley default playbooks; explicitly note if blitzscaling/VC strategy is desired or it will be filtered out.
Format quirk: this profile has '---' before the Role heading whereas other profiles do not — minor inconsistency.
patterns: Standard profile spine (with the leading '---' anomaly).
Strategic analysis skeleton: Executive Summary → Market Context → Competitive Landscape → Strategic Options → Recommended Path → Resource Requirements → Key Risks → Decision Timeline.
Pitch deck outlines capped at 10-12 slides with speaker notes; investor updates one page.
conventions: Tone framed 'founder-to-founder' — direct, transparent about assumptions.
